failure.consequence: The wrong identity would start collection, storage, analysis, and user-visible synthesis against an unrelated company.

evidence: Version v1 selected an exact-name candidate from the wrong industry.
The candidate list contained a description aligned with the user's product-research context.
The resolver had no reliable abstention policy for unresolved ambiguity.

change.scope: Pass bounded company context and candidate descriptions to a narrow resolver; allow no_match and needs_review before downstream collection.

tradeoffs.latency: One additional small-model decision before expensive collection.
tradeoffs.cost: Higher resolver cost; lower expected cost of wrong-source scraping, storage, multimodal analysis, and trust repair.

conditions: Sample ambiguous and no-match production cases.
Monitor latency, cost, review rate, and user-confirmed identity corrections.
Stop downstream work on invalid or unresolved identity status.

limits: Five constructed cases do not estimate production accuracy.
A passing deterministic identity label does not prove the later ad analysis is grounded or useful.
The record demonstrates the method and does not report Heatseeker production metrics.
